analogy: Think of "Combustion and Flame" through the chapter's own opening images: REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS Do you recall the processes of digestion, circulation and respiration which you have studied in your previous classes? These processes are essential for the survival of every individual. You have also learnt about the process of reproduction in plants.... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Combustion and Flame is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that rEPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS Do you recall the processes of digestion, circulation and respiration which you have studied in your previous classes?

The text explains that these processes are essential for the survival of every individual.

The text explains that you have also learnt about the process of reproduction in plants.

The text explains that reproduction is essential for the continuation of a species.

The text explains that imagine what would have happened if organisms had not reproduced.

The text explains that you will realise that reproduction is very important as it ensures the continuation of similar kinds of individuals, generation after generation.

The text explains that you have already learnt in your previous class about reproduction in plants.

The text explains that in this chapter, we shall learn how reproduction takes place in animals.

The text explains that 6.1 Modes of Reproduction Have you seen the young ones of different animals?

The text explains that try to name some of the young ones by completing Table 6.1 shown in examples at S.

The text explains that you must have seen the young ones of various animals being born.

The text explains that can you tell how chicks and caterpillars are born?

The text explains that do you think that these young ones looked the same before they were born as they do now?

The text explains that just as in plants, there are two modes by which animals reproduce.

The text explains that these are: (i) Sexual reproduction, and (ii) Asexual reproduction.

The text explains that 6.2 Sexual Reproduction Try to recall reproduction in plants which you studied in Class VII.

The text explains that it is the job of the worker bees to maintain the temperature of the hive at around 35°C to incubate the eggs.

The text explains that sometimes if the heart and blood vessels have developed you may even see a red spot.

The text explains that look for any twins in your neighbourhood, or among your friends.

The text explains that find out if the twins are identical or non-identical.

The text explains that also find out why identical twins are always of the same sex?

The text explains that if you know of any story about twins, write it in your own words.

The text explains that for more information on animal reproduction, you can visit : l www.saburchill.com l www.teenshealth.org/teen/sexual-health Did You Know?

The text explains that an interesting organisation is observed in a honey bee hive, a colony of several thousand bees.

The text explains that their main job is to build the hive, look after the young and feed the queen bee adequately to keep her healthy so that she can lay eggs.

The text explains that the fertilised eggs hatch into females, while the unfertilised eggs give rise to males, called drones.

Another source point is that rEPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS Do you recall the processes of digestion, circulation and respiration which you have studied in your previous classes?

Another source point is that these processes are essential for the survival of every individual.

Another source point is that you have also learnt about the process of reproduction in plants.

Another source point is that reproduction is essential for the continuation of a species.

Another source point is that imagine what would have happened if organisms had not reproduced.

Source: NCERT Class 8 Science, Chapter 6: Combustion and Flame

prelims_traps: Do not treat REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS and 6.1 Modes of Reproduction as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Combustion and Flame; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Combustion and Flame through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS REPRODUCTION IN ANIMALS and 6.1 Modes of Reproduction to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
